creator: daeron@shadowmere.student.utwente.nl
text:
On Mon, Oct 30, 2000 at 07:26:26AM -0600, mdharnois@home.com wrote:
> 
> >Description:
> 
>       (gdb) run
>       Starting program: /usr/X11R6/lib/mozilla/./mozilla-bin 
>       (no debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...
>       (no debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...
>       (no debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...
>       (no debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...
>       (no debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...
>       (no debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...
>       Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
>       0x2831d596 in pthread_mutex_lock () from /usr/lib/libc_r.so.4
>       

Ok ... This sounds like you suffer from the libgcc_r bug that was recently
discovered about the threads-library not being initialized in time ... I
would suggest you try to update your system to a more recent current, that
has this issue fixed and see if your problem persists.

(My Mozilla Tinderbox Build is working just fine as it is)
> 
-- 
  Pascal Hofstee      < daeron @ shadowmere . student . utwente . nl >
